html:
No rotation: reference frame
Here, all Euler angles are set to zero. The image plane is aligned with the global axes, and the projection of the 3D point P lands at p directly on the plane. This is the standard, unrotated camera reference.
⚠ Figure failed to load

plan.contextChunk:
consecutive frames. After a small time interval, $\Delta t$, the camera
will move, generating a displacement in the points with respect to the
camera coordinate system equal to:

$$\mathbf{P}_{t+\Delta t} = - \mathbf{T}_{\Delta t}  + \mathbf{R}_{\Delta t} \mathbf{P}_t
$$
where $\mathbf{T}_{\Delta t}$ is the camera translation and
$\mathbf{R}_{\Delta t}$ is the camera rotation that took place over that
time interval, $\Delta t$. The velocity of a 3D point with respect to
the camera will be:
$$
\dot{\mathbf{P}} = \frac{\mathbf{P}_{t+\Delta t} - \mathbf{P}_{t}}{\Delta t} = - \mathbf{V} + \frac{\mathbf{R}_{\Delta t} -\mathbf{I}}{\Delta t} \mathbf{P}_t
$${#eq-general_camera_motion_equation_full}

To derive the rotation, we consider the **Euler angles**
(@fig-yaw_pitch_roll) and decompose the rotation using rotations along
the three axes (yaw, pitch, roll):

![Rotation expressed by Euler angles (yaw, pitch, roll).](figures/optical_flow/yaw_pitch_roll.png){width="40%" #fig-yaw_pitch_roll}

Each angle measures the rotation along the camera-coordinate axes. Using
this representation of the rotation, the rotation matrix can be written
as: 
$$\begin{aligned}
\mathbf{R}_{\Delta t} = 
\begin{bmatrix}
\cos \theta_Z &  \sin \theta_Z & 0\\
-\sin \theta_Z &  \cos \theta_Z & 0\\
0 &  0 & 1
\end{bmatrix}
\begin{bmatrix}
\cos \theta_Y &  0 & -\sin \theta_Y\\
0 &  1 & 0\\
\sin \theta_Y &  0 & \cos \theta_Y

elements: 3D axes labeled X, Y, Z intersecting at origin (0,0,0)
Red rotation arrows about each axis labeled Pitch (X), Yaw (Y), Roll (Z)
Euler angle symbols: θ_X, θ_Y, θ_Z near red arrows
Plane perpendicular to Z-axis, shaded gray, representing the image or reference plane
2D in-plane axes (x, y) shown in green on image plane
Point P in space (blue, labeled) with dashed line projecting to plane at point p (blue, labeled)
Dashed lines showing projection from 3D point P to 2D point p
All major axes, labels, and coordinate notations (including 'Yaw', 'Pitch', 'Roll')

interactions.label: Yaw θ_Y (deg)
interactions.teaches: Rotates the camera about the Y axis, demonstrating how horizontal rotations (yaw) change the image plane's orientation and the projection of 3D points.

interactions.label: Pitch θ_X (deg)
interactions.teaches: Rotates the camera about the X axis, showing how tilting up or down (pitch) affects the frame and the position of projected points.

interactions.label: Roll θ_Z (deg)
interactions.teaches: Rotates the camera about the Z axis, illustrating how in-plane rotation (roll) rotates the image axes and projected points around the optical axis.

interactions.label: Show 3D Point Projection
interactions.teaches: Toggles visualization of how a 3D point is projected onto the image plane as the camera rotates.

demo_steps.title: No rotation: reference frame
demo_steps.narration: Here, all Euler angles are set to zero. The image plane is aligned with the global axes, and the projection of the 3D point P lands at p directly on the plane. This is the standard, unrotated camera reference.

demo_steps.title: Apply yaw: horizontal spin
demo_steps.narration: Increasing yaw (θ_Y) turns the camera left or right. Watch as the image plane rotates around the Y axis. The projection of P shifts horizontally on the plane, mimicking a panoramic sweep.

demo_steps.title: Pitch: look up/down
demo_steps.narration: Now reset yaw and increase pitch (θ_X). The camera tilts up, rotating about the X axis. The image plane tilts backward, and the projection of point P moves vertically as if the camera is looking skyward.

demo_steps.title: Roll: rotate image plane
demo_steps.narration: Setting roll (θ_Z) spins the camera about its optical axis, rotating the image itself. The green image axes (x, y) rotate in the plane, but the origin stays fixed. Notice how the projected point p rotates along with the image axes.

demo_steps.title: All rotations combined
demo_steps.narration: With all three angles set, the camera orientation is completely changed: yaw, pitch, and roll act in sequence. The image plane is now slanted in space, and the projection of P can shift both horizontally and vertically, illustrating the combined effects of Euler angle rotations.

demo_steps.title: Hide projection lines
demo_steps.narration: Disable the projection lines to focus only on the orientation of the camera and the meaning of roll, pitch, and yaw rotations relative to the world axes.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: 3D oblique view from above and slightly right, so all three global axes, image plane, and rotations are clearly visible. Ensure adequate zoom to see point P and its projection.
interactionPlan.notes: Use separate colored arrows with circular arcs to represent the direction and sense of each rotation. Overlay angle annotations that update live with sliders. Draw projection lines and points only if showProjection is enabled. For image plane and axes, use a slightly transparent fill and vivid colors for axes; animate both camera and 3D geometry smoothly between demo steps.

attempts.html:
No rotation: reference frame
Here, all Euler angles are set to zero. The image plane is aligned with the global axes, and the projection of the 3D point P lands at p directly on the plane.
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The font size and placement of 'Yaw', 'Pitch', and 'Roll' labels do not match the prominence and position in the source figure.
The green x and y axes vectors on the plane are present but do not match the proportion/length relationship in the original.
The vector for P and p are correct, but the line connecting P-to-p does not mimic the style (dashed/solid) and thickness of the textbook.
Rotation arrows (Yaw, Pitch, Roll) are present but have different start/end points and radii, so curves do not overlay as in the source.
The annotation for axes (X, Y, Z) is less prominent, and the source's axis/thick arrow styling is not replicated.
evaluation.notes: Main geometry, axes, labels, and key interactions are present and highly functional, but visual fidelity on label emphasis, arrow thickness, and axis scale could be improved.
action_items: Increase the font size and boldness of 'Yaw', 'Pitch', 'Roll', and axis (X, Y, Z) labels to match the visual prominence of the source figure.
Adjust the length, proportion, and thickness of main axis arrows, rotation curves, and image-plane axes to more closely match the source.
Refine the style (color, weight, or dash/solid) of the projection and construction lines to better reflect the original illustration.
Fine-tune label placements for clearer alignment with the figure elements as displayed in the source.
Slightly increase the image-plane area for a closer proportion to the reference.

feedback.rationale: All action items and failure modes pertain to visual styles, proportions, line/label styling, and layout details, indicating the plan is correct but further generator refinement is needed for fidelity.
action_items: Increase the font size and boldness of 'Yaw', 'Pitch', 'Roll', and axis (X, Y, Z) labels to match the visual prominence of the source figure.
Adjust the length, proportion, and thickness of main axis arrows, rotation curves, and image-plane axes to more closely match the source.
Refine the style (color, weight, or dash/solid) of the projection and construction lines to better reflect the original illustration.
Fine-tune label placements for clearer alignment with the figure elements as displayed in the source.
Slightly increase the image-plane area for a closer proportion to the reference.

attempts.html:
No rotation: reference frame
Here, all Euler angles are set to zero. The image plane is aligned with the global axes, and the projection of the 3D point P lands at p directly on the plane. This is the standard, unrotated camera reference.
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The red arrows for Yaw, Pitch, and Roll rotations are visually less smooth and shorter than in the source image.
Green axis arrows and black global axes are rendered as 3D arrows rather than 2D, which adds unnecessary perspective effects.
The projected point p and point P are present but p appears with a more prominent outline and is placed slightly more to the right than in the source.
The label '(0,0,0)' is present but less bold and slightly misaligned compared to the source.
The GUI includes step labels and sliders not present in the source figure image.
evaluation.notes: All major elements and rotations are implemented with correct labels and meaningful interactivity, but axes and arcs are more 3D and step controls are extra.
action_items: Refine the appearance of the red rotation arcs to be smoother, longer, and visually more similar to the 2D textbook appearance.
Make the green/yellow axes and black global axes visually flatter by reducing 3D perspective, so they appear more like diagram arrows.
Tweak the positioning and sizing of p, P, and '(0,0,0)' to match the proportions of the source image more closely.
Remove or minimize extra step controls and UI elements that do not appear in the original figure.

feedback.rationale: All conceptual elements and interactions are correct, but discrepancies stem from implementation details like arrow aesthetics, axis perspective, label alignment, and UI clutter, which are generator-level issues.
action_items: Refine the appearance of the red rotation arcs to be smoother, longer, and visually more similar to the 2D textbook appearance.
Make the green/yellow axes and black global axes visually flatter by reducing 3D perspective, so they appear more like diagram arrows.
Tweak the positioning and sizing of p, P, and '(0,0,0)' to match the proportions of the source image more closely.
Remove or minimize extra step controls and UI elements that do not appear in the original figure.

⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The label for Z axis is not angled upward like in the source image.
The 'Pitch' and 'Yaw' labels are placed slightly further from the arcs than in the original.
Rotational arcs are sharp (segmental), not freehand curves as in the hand-drawn image.
The projection drop lines from P to the Z and Y axes are not present in the original.
There are extra step buttons and interactions not seen in the source figure.
evaluation.notes: Strong interactive match with all geometry and labels present, just minor placement and graphic style differences.
action_items: Adjust the Z axis label orientation to better match the original slanted position.
Move Pitch and Yaw labels closer to their arcs for clarity and faithfulness.
Refine arc aesthetics to more closely mimic hand-drawn style from the textbook figure.
Remove the extra projection drop lines and guides to align with the original.
Consider making the interactive step/narration panel collapsible or optional for static-matching mode.

feedback.rationale: All issues are implementation-level (label positions, arc style, removal of extras) and the concept is correct—these can be fixed by refining generation without changing the plan.
